assertions.evidence: response.md: markdown table present with columns Scenario, Shipping Type, Order Value, Destination, Cost?.

assertions.text: Express shipping is represented as at least two distinct rows or cases: one where destination is irrelevant (paid tier, order under £50) and one where UK is specified (free tier, order £50+)
assertions.evidence: response.md: 'Express, large order | Express | £50 or over | [blank] | £0' — destination is blank for the free tier row, treating it as worldwide. The note reinforces the error: 'Express shipping is free for orders of £50 and over, regardless of destination.' This contradicts the rule that the free tier is UK-only. The UK-specific free tier is never represented as a separate case; the destination asymmetry between the paid and free tiers is not noticed.

assertions.text: Standard shipping destination is blank or absent from the table — it is genuinely irrelevant and should not be filled with a dummy value
assertions.evidence: response.md: 'Standard shipping | Standard | any | [blank] | £3.99' — destination cell is blank.

assertions.text: Overnight shipping rows specify UK and/or Ireland as destinations — not left blank, since availability depends on it
assertions.evidence: response.md: 'Overnight shipping, UK | Overnight | any | UK | £14.99' and 'Overnight shipping, Ireland | Overnight | any | Ireland | £14.99' — both destinations explicitly specified.

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
